# UC-23.6.4 · Payment Processing Success Rate and Revenue Leakage

## Description

Detects deterioration of blended card-not-present authorization-rate inside heterogeneous PSP telemetry normalized into Splunk — Stripe, Adyen, Braintree, Worldpay (FIS), Cybersource (Visa), Authorize.Net, and PayPal — while preserving per-vendor refusal semantics: ISO acquirer digits (`51`, `05`, `54`, `61`, `65`, `41`, `43`, `78`, …) merged with PSP textual synonyms via `card_decline_codes.csv`, separates stepped-up Strong Customer Authentication friction from issuer-coded declines, tracks EMV three-domain secure challenge-timeout abandonment proxies, contrasts intentional fraud-screen stops against retry-eligible soft declines, and estimates salvageable GMV leakage using modeled recovery-failure probabilities rather than labeling every refusal as permanently lost basket revenue.

## Value

Stripe State-of-Payments commentary positioned beside merchant benchmarking surveys stresses that roughly one-point authorization-rate uplift typically yields roughly four million to seven million dollars incremental annual revenue per billion dollars gross merchandise volume — revenue-leakage often hides inside ignored soft-decline retry ladders rather than headline fraud dashboards — while PSD2 Regulatory Technical Standards strong-customer-authentication expectations applied across European Economic Area corridors since September twenty nineteen still demand stepped-up verification beyond modest thresholds unless documented exemptions (low-value baskets, recurring mandates, trusted beneficiaries, corporate corridors) stay provably coded; Chief Financial Officer, Chief Information Security Officer, and Product executives therefore co-own blended authorization-rate KPIs alongside fraud-ratio tiles because blunt velocity controls silently bleed GMV comparable to acquisition budgets, and Visa interchange programmes gate standard tiers below roughly nine tenths of one percent chargeback ratios — exceeding them pulls merchants into monitored remediation calendars regardless of PSP uptime badges.

## Implementation

(1) Issue Splunk HTTP Event Collector tokens per PSP sourcetype family into `index=business`; enforce webhook signing validation upstream of Splunk ingestion. (2) Publish `card_decline_codes.csv` mapping ISO numeric acquirer responses (`51`, `05`, `54`, etc.) plus Stripe textual synonyms onto soft-hard taxonomy and salvage fractions Finance accepts. (3) Schedule hourly authorization-rate panels comparing PSP cohorts versus ninety-six percent aspirational benchmark for card-not-present excellence. (4) Alert when blended hourly authorization-rate drops twelve percentage points beneath trailing twenty-eight-day median per PSP-region tuple while suppressing planned issuer maintenance windows stored in lookup overlays. (5) Partner Fraud Operations so Decision Manager style declines reconcile against Splunk decline buckets weekly.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Splunk Enterprise or Splunk Cloud search tier aligned with splunkVersions; Heavy Forwarder or ingestion tier able to terminate TLS for PSP webhooks and optional scripted REST pollers toward Stripe (`api.stripe.com`), Adyen Checkout (`checkout-test.adyen.com` / live), Braintree (`api.braintreegateway.com`), PayPal (`api.paypal.com`), Cybersource REST (`apitest.cybersource.com`), Authorize.Net reporting SOAP or REST endpoints, and Worldpay Card-Online routes Finance sanctions.
• Splunk roles restricting `index=business` payment-detail lines to Revenue Operations, Payments Engineering, Fraud Operations, and designated Finance analysts — PAN fields must never land raw in Splunk; tokenize or truncate before indexing per PCI DSS scope agreements even when this UC focuses on authorization-rate math rather than storage audits.
• Lookup governance: publish `card_decline_codes.csv` under Splunk lookups with columns `iso_numeric`, `decline_meaning`, `soft_hard_typical`, `recover_pct` sourced from Visa acquirer guides plus Stripe decline taxonomy mapping — Finance Legal approves rounding assumptions used inside leakage formulas.
• Optional JDBC warehouse projections via Splunk DB Connect ([Splunkbase 2686](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2686)) holding PSP reconciliation settlement calendars (`settlement_batch_date`, `capture_delay_days`) used only for explanatory overlays, not as primary webhook telemetry.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Stand up Splunk HTTP Event Collector tokens scoped per PSP family — Heavy Forwarder `inputs.conf` stanza naming indexes default `business`, sourcetypes exactly `stripe:event`, `adyen:notification`, `braintree:event`, `worldpay:payment`, `cybersource:payment`, `authorize_net:txn`, `paypal:webhook`. Stripe: validate `Stripe-Signature` HMAC-SHA256 against rolling webhook secrets for topics including `payment_intent.succeeded`, `payment_intent.payment_failed`, `payment_intent.requires_action`, `charge.succeeded`, `charge.failed`, `charge.refunded`, `charge.dispute.created`. Adyen: ingest HMAC-signed JSON notifications carrying event codes such as AUTHORISATION, CAPTURE, CAPTURE_FAILED, REFUND, CHARGEBACK with `merchantReference` plus `pspReference`. Braintree: map webhook payloads (`transaction_settled`, `transaction_settlement_declined`) into normalized JSON lines with processor-response codes preserved. PayPal: subscribe REST webhooks `PAYMENT.CAPTURE.COMPLETED`, `PAYMENT.CAPTURE.DENIED`, `PAYMENT.CAPTURE.PENDING`. Authorize.Net: schedule modular inputs calling Transaction Reporting API `getTransactionDetailsRequest`, projecting transaction IDs into `authorize_net:txn`. Cybersource and Worldpay REST pulls poll merchant consoles when webhook gaps appear — stamp `_time` from PSP decision timestamps rather than indexer receipt during reconciliation drills.

Mirror settlement warehouse slices through Splunk DB Connect JDBC hourly batches landing companion sourcetypes such as `dbx:psp_settlement_calendar` keyed by `merchant_id` and `psp` so Splunk dashboards annotate T-plus-two versus T-plus-three batches without pretending webhook latency equals funds availability.

Optional Salesforce Commerce Cloud linkage: Splunk Add-on for Salesforce ([Splunkbase 3549](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/3549)) when order identifiers originate there — align `order_id` fields feeding `txn_key` coalesce chains.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Paste the canonical SPL from the parent `spl` field into Search and Reporting; macro-wrap thresholds (`stripe_benchmark_floor`, `margin_pct_gate`) inside `macros.conf` so Revenue Operations adjusts floors without rewriting tokens weekly.

Save scheduled report `payments_auth_rate_hourly`; attach alert comparing rolling hourly authorization-rate per PSP against Stripe benchmark bands (ninety-five to ninety-seven percent for card-not-present excellence) with suppression keys `psp` plus `merchant_region` tuples across ninety minutes to absorb transient spikes.

Clone an optional Splunk IT Service Intelligence KPI (`premiumApps`) forwarding blended authorization-rate metrics into service analyzers where ecommerce checkout maps to named ITSI services — keeps PSP outages aligned with broader digital storefront episodes without forcing this UC through unrelated finance JDBC dashboards.

Step 3 — Pipeline mechanics auditors care about
The opening predicate unions heterogeneous PSP feeds while preserving vendor-native codes — governance insists sourcetype naming stays stable even when Stripe duplicates retries across Smart Retries windows.

`txn_key` collapses divergent identifiers (`payment_intent_id`, `psp_reference`, `gateway_txn_ref`) so finance compares Splunk aggregates against PSP consoles without rewriting SPL whenever ERP migrations rename columns upstream.

Lookup enrichment attaches ISO-inspired decline semantics (`decline_meaning`, `recover_pct`) enabling soft versus hard leakage splits — fraud analysts tune `recover_pct` quarterly using offline experiments measuring salvage capture after intelligent retries.

`authorized_flag` collapses webhook verbs plus normalized booleans — null outcomes remain excluded via `where isnotnull(authorized_flag)` so partially parsed backlog rows never silently count as approvals.

`challenge_timeout` doubles as PSD2 Strong Customer Authentication friction telemetry — spikes correlate with abandonment overlays sourced separately from storefront telemetry rather than interpreted purely as issuer declines.

`stats … by psp brand region` exposes merchant-facing dimensional cuts leadership expects inside quarterly business reviews alongside CFO-ready commentary.

Step 4 — Validate and operationalize
Daily, compare Splunk `success_rate` per PSP against Stripe Dashboard charts and Adyen Customer Area KPI panels for identical merchant accounts — tolerate rounding deltas under fifty basis points before escalating engineering bridges.

Weekly, reconcile soft-decline dollar totals (`revenue_leakage_soft`) against Finance spreadsheets estimating unrealized recoveries — discrepancies trace to stale `recover_pct` assumptions rather than ingestion outages.

Quarterly, rotate webhook secrets across PSP portals and confirm Splunk `splunkd.log` lacks sustained HTTP four-o-three spikes indicative of stale signing keys.

Publish Dashboard Studio workbook layering line charts for PSP authorization-rate trends, gauge visuals comparing blended rate versus ninety-six percent aspirational target, Sankey diagrams depicting initiated versus authorized versus captured splits, heatmaps plotting decline buckets against brand-region tuples, tabular rankings of leakage dollars prioritized by salvage feasibility — tie drill-through URLs back into PSP consoles via encoded macros storing merchant descriptors safely outside PAN scope.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting and misleading signals
• Acquirer BIN routing optimizations shifting issuer paths intra-day or intra-month often spike declines originating from issuer desks while the gateway remained healthy — correlate against acquirer bulletins before blaming PSP firmware.

• PSD2 Regulatory Technical Standards exemptions misapplied for low-value baskets under thirty euros or trusted-beneficiary flows may trigger needless Strong Customer Authentication challenges — Splunk friction spikes resemble issuer declines though checkout logic owns the fix.

• Digital-wallet token handshake failures at Apple Pay or Google Pay layers sometimes cascade shoppers back into keyed-entry plastics, temporarily depressing authorization-rate until retries succeed — bifurcate wallet cohorts before reacting.

• Stripe Smart Retries deliberately postpone salvage captures across multi-day horizons — attempt-one dashboards look bleak while eventual recoveries settle downstream — align KPI windows with reconciliation calendars before interpreting leakage math literally.

• Issuers undergoing planned maintenance overnight Sundays across European corridors or Pacific mornings emit clustered soft declines resembling outages — overlay calendar feeds where Visa publishes issuer maintenance advisories.

• Gateway thirty-second timeouts racing issuer approvals occasionally record ambiguous declines despite eventual approvals arriving milliseconds later — shorten noisy comparisons using PSP reconciliation APIs rather than webhook arrival ordering alone.

• Network-token migrations mixing Visa Token Service credentials against legacy PAN hashes introduce duplicate declines until merchants finish wallet provisioning — monitor rollout milestones alongside Splunk spikes.

• Stale BIN directories missing freshly allocated issuer ranges classify legitimate plastics as invalid until quarterly BIN-file refreshes publish — spikes concentrate on niche BIN slices rather than uniform issuer degradation.

• Dynamic Currency Conversion opt-ins prepend conversion deltas that confuse ERP pairing — mismatch declines resemble authorization faults though treasury reconciliation clears variance once FX spreads understood.

• Prepaid plastics routinely exhibit twenty-five to thirty-five percent lower authorization-rate baselines versus revolving credit cohorts — segmentation avoids misguided fraud pushes against lawful prepaid-heavy demographics.

• Recurring subscriptions relying on vaulted credentials invoke exemption pathways differing from one-off guest checkout Strong Customer Authentication demands — blend ratios skew when marketing launches flash promotions atop recurring cohorts without tagging intent.

• Dispute webhook storms mixing friendly fraud versus stolen credential abuse require downstream dispute tooling — Splunk dispute counts alone cannot adjudicate fraud typologies absent manual chargeback coding feeds.

• Settlement calendars spanning T-plus-zero through T-plus-three batches misalign webhook timestamps versus treasury cash postings — finance variance reviews belong to settlement dashboards, not authorization-rate alerts alone.

• PSP-wide degraded incidents occasionally emit issuer-coded declines although gateway faults prevented submissions — cross-reference vendor status pages before initiating issuer escalation bridges lasting beyond contractual merchant thresholds.

## SPL

```spl
index=business (sourcetype="stripe:event" OR sourcetype="adyen:notification" OR sourcetype="braintree:event" OR sourcetype="worldpay:payment" OR sourcetype="cybersource:payment" OR sourcetype="authorize_net:txn" OR sourcetype="paypal:webhook") earliest=-24h
| eval txn_key=tostring(coalesce(payment_intent_id,charge_id,psp_reference,payment_id,transaction_id,order_id,gateway_txn_ref,""))
| eval psp=case(sourcetype=="stripe:event","stripe",sourcetype=="adyen:notification","adyen",sourcetype=="braintree:event","braintree",sourcetype=="worldpay:payment","worldpay",sourcetype=="cybersource:payment","cybersource",sourcetype=="authorize_net:txn","authorize_net",sourcetype=="paypal:webhook","paypal",1=1,"unknown")
| eval amt=tonumber(coalesce(amount,capture_amount,txn_amount_minor,value,"0"))
| eval stripe_decl=lower(tostring(coalesce(decline_code,outcome_reason,outcome_detail,"")))
| eval iso_digits=tostring(trim(coalesce(network_decline_code,processor_response_code,issuer_response_code,cvv_response_code,"")))
| eval iso_digits=if(match(iso_digits,"^[0-9]{2}$"),iso_digits,null())
| eval unified_iso=coalesce(iso_digits,case(stripe_decl=="insufficient_funds","51",stripe_decl=="expired_card","54",stripe_decl=="lost_card","41",stripe_decl=="stolen_card","43",stripe_decl=="do_not_honor","05",stripe_decl=="generic_decline","05",stripe_decl=="processing_error","91",1=1,null()))
| lookup card_decline_codes.csv iso_numeric AS unified_iso OUTPUT decline_meaning soft_hard_typical recover_pct
| eval recover_eff=coalesce(recover_pct,0.35)
| eval recovery_failure_rate=round(1-recover_eff,4)
| eval soft_hard=coalesce(soft_hard_typical,if(match(stripe_decl,"insufficient_funds|do_not_honor|generic_decline|processing_error"),"soft",if(match(stripe_decl,"lost_card|stolen_card|expired_card|fraudulent"),"hard","unknown")))
| eval evt_norm=lower(tostring(coalesce(event_type,type,notification_event,"")))
| where NOT match(evt_norm,"charge\.refunded|payment\.refund\.completed")
| eval authorized_flag=case(match(evt_norm,"payment_intent\.succeeded|charge\.succeeded|payment\.capture\.completed"),1,match(evt_norm,"payment_intent\.payment_failed|charge\.failed|capture_failed|capture\.denied"),0,match(lower(tostring(coalesce(success_flag,outcome_status,auth_result,""))),"true|authorised|authorized|captured|settled"),1,match(lower(tostring(coalesce(success_flag,outcome_status,auth_result,""))),"false|refused|declined|denied"),0,1=1,null())
| where isnotnull(authorized_flag)
| eval challenge_timeout=if(match(evt_norm,"requires_action|challenge_timeout|challenge_abandon") OR match(lower(tostring(coalesce(reason_code,""))),"authentication_required"),1,0)
| eval region=tostring(coalesce(merchant_region,billing_country,country_iso,shopper_locale_country,"UNK"))
| eval brand=tostring(coalesce(card_brand,card_scheme,payment_method_brand,network_brand,"UNK"))
| eval decline_amt=if(authorized_flag=0,amt,0)
| eval revenue_leakage_soft=if(authorized_flag=0 AND soft_hard=="soft", decline_amt*recovery_failure_rate, 0)
| stats sum(eval(if(authorized_flag=1,1,0))) as auths_ok count as attempts sum(revenue_leakage_soft) as revenue_leakage_soft sum(eval(if(challenge_timeout=1,amt,0))) as challenge_amt sum(eval(if(authorized_flag=0 AND soft_hard=="hard",amt,0))) as hard_decline_amt by psp brand region
| eval success_rate=round(100*auths_ok/nullif(attempts,0),2)
| sort - revenue_leakage_soft
| table psp brand region attempts success_rate revenue_leakage_soft challenge_amt hard_decline_amt
```

## Visualization

Dashboard Studio sequence featuring line charts trending blended authorization-rate per PSP versus trailing baseline; radial gauge comparing portfolio authorization-rate against ninety-five through ninety-seven percent Stripe benchmark corridor for card-not-present leaders; Sankey visualization plotting shopper funnel stages Initiated through Authorized toward Captured versus Declined branches sourced from webhook verbs; heatmaps colouring decline buckets across card-brand versus merchant-region grids; prioritized tables enumerating salvage-eligible leakage dollars ranked by modeled recovery uplift.
